name: tailwind-warning-auto-fix
description: Automatically fixes every Tailwind CSS IntelliSense optimization warning in the active file with one command — no more clicking Quick Fix one class at a time.

tailwindAutoOptimizer.confirmBeforeApply.description: Show a confirmation dialog before applying optimization fixes.

tailwindAutoOptimizer.showSummary.description: Show a summary notification after Tailwind: Fix All Warnings finishes.

tailwindAutoOptimizer.autoFixOnSave.description: Automatically fix Tailwind optimization warnings whenever a file is saved, silently and with no confirmation dialog. Class conflicts are never auto-resolved on save — see conflictResolutionStrategy for manual runs.

enumDescriptions: Ask which class to keep for each unique conflicting pair (default, safest).

Automatically keep whichever class appears FIRST in the markup for every conflict, no prompt. NOT guaranteed to match what actually renders — Tailwind's real precedence depends on its internal stylesheet order, not markup order. Can silently change page appearance.
Automatically keep whichever class appears LAST in the markup for every conflict, no prompt. Same accuracy risk as keepFirst, just the opposite choice.
Never touch class conflicts automatically — they remain visible in the Problems panel only.
tailwindAutoOptimizer.conflictResolution.description: How to resolve Tailwind class conflicts (e.g. 'text-left' vs 'text-center') when running Fix All Warnings. The default, 'ask', shows one confirmation per UNIQUE conflicting pair (repeats of the same pair reuse your answer automatically). The 'keepFirst'/'keepLast' options remove the review step entirely but are NOT guaranteed to keep the class that's actually rendering — use only if you accept that trade-off.
